component.name: FileUploader
component.description: A dashed dropzone that opens the native file picker on click and accepts drag-and-drop, paired with a list of file rows showing each file's name, size, upload progress, and a remove control. Exports both FileUploader (dropzone + list) and FileRow (a single file item — the Figma isEmpty=false part).
component.note: Dropzone is a clickable DIV (not a button) because it wraps a Button; keyboard/AT users trigger the hidden native <input type=file> via the inner Button. Drag-over tints the zone forest.50 + switches the dashed border to SOLID forest.500. UploadFile = { id, name, size?, progress? (0–1) }. Reuses Button, Icon, and ProgressBar. Synced to Figma '❖ File Uploader'.

dropzone.background: colors.background.neutral.default (input white); hover → colors.background.primary.weak (forest.50); dragging → colors.background.primary.weak (forest.50); disabled → colors.background.neutral.weak (neutral.50)
dropzone.border: 1px dashed colors.border.neutral.default (neutral.300); hover → forest.500; dragging → SOLID colors.border.primary.default (forest.500)

tokens.input: visually-hidden native <input type=file> (1px clip) — keeps keyboard + a11y intact

progress.bar: reused ProgressBar (value 0–1); appearance='success' at progress ≥ 1 (still forest — forest IS the system green)

useCases: Let a user attach one or many files to a form (resumes, documents, images)
Upload media or assets with visible per-file progress
Import a dataset or file inside a settings or admin flow
Collect attachments inside a Modal/dialog upload step
Replace/add to an existing set of files, showing the current list below the zone
requiredProps: label (or aria-label on the input) for an accessible name

commonPatterns.name: Form attachment field
commonPatterns.description: Controlled uploader inside a form section; files held in state, added via onFilesAdded, removed via onRemove.
commonPatterns.jsx: <FileUploader label='Attachments' message='PDF or PNG, up to 10MB' multiple files={files} onFilesAdded={add} onRemove={remove} />

commonPatterns.name: Image upload with placeholder
commonPatterns.description: Use the image placeholder icon and an accept filter for image-only uploads.
commonPatterns.jsx: <FileUploader label='Cover image' icon='image' accept='image/*' buttonLabel='Add image' onFilesAdded={add} />

commonPatterns.name: Determinate progress list
commonPatterns.description: Each in-flight file carries a progress 0–1; the FileRow bar reads as success at 1.
commonPatterns.jsx: <FileUploader files={[{ id: '1', name: 'report.pdf', size: '200 KB', progress: 0.6 }]} onFilesAdded={add} onRemove={remove} />

commonPatterns.name: Standalone file row
commonPatterns.description: Render a single already-uploaded file outside the uploader (e.g. in a summary).
commonPatterns.jsx: <FileRow file={{ id: '1', name: 'logo.svg', size: '12 KB' }} onRemove={remove} />

antiPatterns.scenario: Using drag-and-drop as the only upload path
antiPatterns.reason: Drag-only excludes keyboard, touch, and assistive-tech users; the zone must always be click/Button-triggerable.
antiPatterns.alternative: Keep the always-present click-to-upload Button; treat drag-and-drop as an enhancement on top of it.

antiPatterns.scenario: Omitting the field label and the input's aria-label
antiPatterns.reason: The hidden native input has no accessible name and is unusable by screen readers.
antiPatterns.alternative: Always pass label (it is wired as aria-labelledby), or set an aria-label on the control.

antiPatterns.scenario: Showing a determinate percent when upload progress is unknown
antiPatterns.reason: A frozen or fabricated percent misleads users about real upload state.
antiPatterns.alternative: Use an indeterminate progress pattern until a real fraction is available; omit progress for already-uploaded files.

antiPatterns.scenario: Using FileUploader for a single inline avatar / image picker
antiPatterns.reason: The full dropzone + list is heavy for a one-shot single-image swap.
antiPatterns.alternative: Use a simple Button + preview thumbnail for lightweight single-avatar pickers.

slots.label: Optional field label above the dropzone (12/16 semibold neutral.600); wired as the input's accessible name
slots.buttonLabel: Text inside the dropzone Button (default 'Upload file')
slots.message: Hint under the Button — accepted types / max size (12/16 neutral.500)
slots.icon: 48px placeholder icon in the zone: image (default) | cloud-upload | file

interactions.click: Clicking anywhere in the zone OR the inner Button opens the hidden native file input; the Button stops propagation so it triggers the picker once
interactions.hover: Zone tints forest.50 and the dashed border switches to forest.500
interactions.dragOver: Sets a dragging state — zone tints forest.50 and the border becomes SOLID forest.500 (Figma isSelected)
interactions.dragLeave: Clears dragging; ignores leaves into child elements
interactions.drop: Clears dragging and emits the dropped files via onFilesAdded(File[])
interactions.select: onChange emits chosen files via onFilesAdded(File[]); input value is reset so the same file can be re-selected
interactions.remove: Each FileRow trash button fires onRemove(id)
interactions.disabled: Zone cursor not-allowed, neutral.50 fill, muted placeholder; drag/drop/click are ignored and the input is disabled

behavior.controlled: files is a controlled list owned by the parent; onFilesAdded(File[]) and onRemove(id) drive it. progress (0–1) per file is also parent-controlled.
behavior.progress: FileRow renders the reused ProgressBar with value 0–1; at progress ≥ 1 it switches to appearance='success' (still forest).

accessibility.role: file input (native <input type=file>) drives the control and its accessible name
accessibility.accessibleName: aria-labelledby points at the field label; provide a label (or aria-label) so the control is named
accessibility.hiddenInput: The native input is visually-hidden (1px clip), NOT display:none — keeping it keyboard-focusable and exposed to AT
accessibility.zoneSemantics: The dropzone is a clickable DIV (it wraps a Button), so keyboard/AT users trigger uploads via the inner Button + hidden input rather than the div
accessibility.removeButtons: Each FileRow remove button is a real <button> with aria-label 'Remove {name}' (overridable via removeLabel)
accessibility.focusRing: Inner Button shows the focus ring (zone uses focus-within: outline none); remove button uses a 2px forest.700 inset ring
accessibility.dragAndDrop: Drag-and-drop is an enhancement layered over the always-present click-to-upload — never the only path
accessibility.keyboardSupport: Tab to the Button, Enter/Space to open the picker; Tab to each remove button, Enter/Space to remove
accessibility.contrastNote: forest.700 file icon on forest.50 pill, neutral.800 file name, and forest.500 percent on white all meet AA.

athenaConstraints.dropzoneBorder: 1px dashed neutral.300; solid forest.500 on hover/drag
athenaConstraints.borderRadius: radius.none (rows + remove button); radius.pill only on the file icon
athenaConstraints.brandAccent: forest.500 (single brand accent) — used for tint, percent, and success progress; forest IS the system green
athenaConstraints.alwaysClickable: click/Button upload is always present; drag-and-drop is additive only

figmaDivergences.implementation: a clickable DIV (not a button) because it contains a Button; keyboard/AT users trigger via the inner Button + the hidden native input
figmaDivergences.reason: A button cannot legally nest a button; the hidden <input type=file> carries the real control semantics and accessible name.

figmaDivergences.figma: shows a detached progress bar with a percent
figmaDivergences.implementation: a composed reuse of the Athena ProgressBar (value 0–1) plus a forest.500 percent label
figmaDivergences.reason: Reuse the system ProgressBar rather than redrawing the bar; success appearance kicks in at progress ≥ 1.

figmaDivergences.implementation: added three new icons to the set: file, image, cloud-upload
figmaDivergences.reason: The placeholder and file-row pill needed glyphs that did not yet exist in the Icon set.

aiHints.context: Reach for FileUploader when a user attaches or uploads one or many files, especially when per-file progress and a removable list matter. Always keep the click/Button path; layer drag-and-drop as an enhancement. Provide a label (accessible name) and a message hint for accepted types/size. For a lightweight single-image/avatar swap, prefer a Button + preview instead.
neverUseWhen: Drag-and-drop would be the only upload path — keep the click/Button fallback
No label and no aria-label is provided — the input has no accessible name
Upload progress is unknown but you would show a determinate percent — use an indeterminate pattern
A single inline avatar / image picker is enough — a Button + preview is lighter
